name: European Union
plain_words: EU sets VAT rules: minimum standard rate 15%. OSS lets businesses declare VAT in one member state for all EU sales. IOSS for imports under EUR 150. DAC7 requires platforms to report seller income.

taxes.name: VAT Harmonization
taxes.plain_words: EU sets the floor: minimum 15% standard VAT rate. Member states choose their own rates above that. Some have multiple reduced rates (5%, 0%) for specific goods.
taxes.notes: EU Directive 2006/112/EC sets minimum standard rate at 15%. Reduced rates allowed for specific goods/services. Member states set their own rates above the floor.

thresholds.name: oss_threshold_intra_eu
thresholds.notes: Below this, VAT at origin country rate. Above, OSS required.

taxes.name: OSS (One Stop Shop)
taxes.plain_words: One Stop Shop: declare and pay VAT for all EU B2C sales in one member state. No need to register in every country you sell to.
taxes.notes: Covers distance sales, digital services, and marketplace sales. Replaced Mini One Stop Shop (MOSS) from July 2021.

deadlines.name: oss_quarterly
deadlines.detail: File OSS return by end of month after quarter

taxes.name: IOSS (Import One Stop Shop)
taxes.plain_words: Import One Stop Shop: declare and pay VAT on imports valued under EUR 150 in one return. No customs delays for buyers. Simple for sellers.
taxes.notes: Threshold: EUR 150 per consignment. Above 150, normal customs + import VAT applies.

taxes.name: DAC7 (Platform Reporting)
taxes.plain_words: Digital platforms must report seller income to tax authorities. If you sell on a platform, the platform tells the tax office. Threshold: sellers with 30+ sales or EUR 2,000+ must be reported.
taxes.notes: Directive 2021/514 (DAC7). Platforms must report seller identity, income, and bank accounts. Reportable if 30+ sales OR 2000+ EUR in a year.

deadlines.name: platform_report
deadlines.detail: Report by 31 January following the reportable year
deadlines.penalty: Member state specific, up to EUR 10,000 per omission
